title: Run full three-way fleet
description: Scope: after .28, .29, and .34 converge through .30.1, run the full requested Python, Rust, and Zephyr/Renode node counts together in one lichen-sim scenario. Use AWS_PROFILE=personal and AWS_REGION=us-west-2 in account 210337117346, launching only tagged ARM64 fleet AMI ami-0764d1b512e22671f instances. Never attach builder EBS vol-0a95eee8d1d8461eb. Supply the exact clean source base and binary patch, Python/Cargo locks and toolchain, Zephyr ELF, Renode model/platform files, fleet scripts and analyzer because the AMI has tools but no checkout or credentials; hash all local and remote inputs. Python/Rust must use registered clients and every Zephyr node a dedicated RenodeServer endpoint while all nodes share one simulation.
design: Before launch prove account 210337117346, us-west-2, AMI ID/ARM64 architecture, and Project=LICHEN/unique RunId/LaunchedBy/Name tags. Record exact package/upload/launch/build/run/analyze/collect/cleanup commands, cwd/environment, versions, UTC start/end, exit codes, requested/actual node counts, instance IDs, RunId, process logs, strict telemetry JSONL, report and artifact SHA-256 values. Every process must exit successfully and telemetry packet hashes must prove each implementation has TX and RX plus pairwise cross-reception; aggregate counts are insufficient. Missing node, zero counter, malformed/missing telemetry, endpoint collision, timeout, silent retry/reduction, ignored nonzero exit, analyzer/log-copy failure, or hash mismatch fails closed. Copy/hash logs first, recheck account and ownership tags, terminate only recorded instances, and wait terminated. Prove no builder EBS attachment; accidental approved-volume use fails and requires sync/unmount, ownership-checked detach, and wait-volume-available.
acceptance_criteria: Dependency .30.1 is closed after .28, .29, and .34; then exact requested Python/Rust/Zephyr counts run on ami-0764d1b512e22671f in one simulation with registered Python/Rust clients and dedicated Zephyr RenodeServer endpoints. Strict telemetry and packet hashes prove TX/RX for each implementation and every required cross-implementation reception pair; all processes and analyzer exit zero. Complete source/patch/lock/tool/model/command/exit/instance/RunId/log/artifact provenance is retained and hashed. No skip, count reduction, aggregate-only evidence, hidden retry, or cleanup failure passes. Ownership-checked termination/wait succeeds; fleet has no vol-0a95eee8d1d8461eb attachment, with unmount/detach/wait-available mandatory if one occurred.
notes: Preserved original requirement: Python, Rust, and T-Echo Zephyr nodes run together through lichen-sim; per-implementation TX/RX and cross-reception evidence is mandatory and aggregate traffic alone is insufficient.
